subject: Biology
title: Seed Dispersal Strategies
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Seed Dispersal Strategies.

hook.text: A seed that falls under its parent tree will compete for the same light and water. It needs to travel.

buildup.text: Wind carries dandelion and maple seeds. Water carries coconuts across oceans.

discovery.text: Burrs hitch rides on animal fur. Berries travel through animal digestive systems.

twist.text: Fire triggers some seeds to open. Australian banksia seeds only germinate after bushfires.

climax.text: Each dispersal method solves the same problem: get far from home and find space to grow.

punchline.text: Seeds travel by wind, water, fire, and animal.

quiz.question: What triggers Australian banksia seeds to germinate?
